channel.title: Golang on Frank Huang
channel.description: Recent content in Golang on Frank Huang

item.title: Difference in new and make in golang
item.description: new(T) 会申请一个容量是0的空间来存放T类型的数据，并且返回该空间的地址。 // Allocate enough memory to store a bytes.Buffer value // and return a pointer to the value's address. var buf bytes.Buffer p := &buf // Use a composite literal to perform allocation and // return a pointer to the value's address. p := &bytes.Buffer{} // Use the new function to perform allocation, which will // return a pointer to the value's address. p := new(bytes.Buffer) make是专门用来初始化map，slice和channel的，不会返回地址 三种使用make的方式：

item.title: Different ways of copying arrays in golang
item.description: Recently I’ve been using golang as the programming language for solving leetcode problems. I came across with the problems of array copying. func f1(grid [][]int) int { rows := len(grid) var sum = make([][]int, rows) copy(grid, sum) fmt.Printf("%p\n", &sum) fmt.Printf("%p\n", &grid) } // 0xc42000a080 // 0xc42000a060 The result of the above code shows that go actaully assign a new address for the newly created array. But when I try to modify the value inside the sum matrix, the corresponding value of the grid is also changed.

item.title: Strings, bytes, runes and characters in Go
item.description: 在编程的世界里，最主要的 #Reference Strings, bytes, runes and characters in Go

item.title: Golang learning 5 -- Concurrent
item.description: Channel is like a bus transporting data as people from one place to an other while persist them 今天结合最近几天边看书边写GO的多线程应用来讲一下我对golang里面多线程编程的感受。 Channels 在go并发编程的时候，有一个叫做Channel的概念是跟java的并发编程很不一样的。Java里面为了保证变量在多线程中的一致性和防止死锁的情况发生，引入了很多带锁性质的变量，这对于猴子来说是一件很苦恼的事情，有时候莫名其妙就出现死锁，莫名其妙就数据不一致了，这其实要求猴子们对java内置的数据类型非常了解，以至于至今我也还在学习。 但是GO引入了一个Channel的概念，大大的降低了并发编程的难度。下面我们来看看Channel的一些实现和使用方法。The go programming language书中提到了Channel相当于在不同的Goroutine之间架起的桥梁，每一个Channelui有一个变量类型，这个变量类型表示这个Channel只能传输这样的数据类型。 declaration ch := make(chan int, capacity int): This cause the ch to have type chan int which is also a reference. This initialize the ch to equal nil(the null value in GO). capacity determine if the channel is buffered or not(0). 声明一个receive channel可以用chan<- int，声明一个send channel可以用<-chan int.

item.title: Golang learning 4 -- Function
item.description: Handling error 在go里面，个人感觉有几个很有用的包很有用，下面就记录一下。 - use fmt.Errorf(), fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "") or log.Fatalf() to back probagate the error to the caller - If using log.Fatalf() for returning an error, use function like log.SetPrefix() for formatting the log message - EOF error: define EOF using ‘var err := errors.New(“EOF”)’ and check the EOF error type by if err == io.EOF go推荐所有在中间过程产生的error都抛给caller，再链式的返回main函数，所有中间产生error都应该被看作是bug。

item.title: Golang learning 3 -- composite type
item.description: Array 几乎所有的编程语言都是从array开始的，因为array实在是太重要了，基本上很多算法都是基于array来展开的，Go 当然也不例外啦。Array 是定长的，可扩展的集合类型叫_slice_。 initializing array q := […]int{1,2,3} //length are defined by the element number var q = [3]int{1,2} // the third element is 0 // define with index and value pairs type Currency int const ( USD Currency = iota EUR GBP RMB ) symbol := [...]string{USD: "$", EUR: "€", GBP: "£", RMB: "¥"} Notice [3]int{} and [4]int{} are different types which means length are also one of the feature for defining different array types.

item.title: Golang learning 2
item.description: 一个array会包含三个信息：capacity， length还有一个指向数组头部的pointer。 初始化一个map 在go里面，map实际上是hashtable的reference，因此很多hashtable的特性他都具备。go里面的key是唯一的，因此可以作为set来使用。 m := make(map[string] int) 也可以写成 m := map[string] int{} 这是初始化一个empty的map，如果要初始化值，可以在大括号中加入dictionary的key/value。值得注意的是，map并不像普通的array那样，可以用&符号来取得其地址，因为每次加入一个元素的时候都会rehash，所以地址一直在变的。

item.title: Golang learning 1
item.description: Welcome to my journey of learning Golang Starting from today, I will write a post describing my journey of learning Golang by reading [The Go Programming Language](). It is a long book though, so I will try my best to keep updating the post about my learning on Golang everyday. As a kick off post, I don’t want to start with the hello world application, I want to point out some syntax that is new for Go comparing other language that I have used like JAVA and Python.
